title: Battlefield of the Mother
excerpt: For centuries soldiers have gone into battle and women have gone into motherhood with equal courage. Like the battlefield, birth is bloody and intense. Women shouldn’t have to face it alone, and thankfully seldom do.
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: The Funeral
excerpt: A story of those I have lost along the way and the importance of always going to the funeral.

excerpt: Romance is not something out of a Disney Movie. It is raw, it is real, it is Divine.

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:
Postpartum Resource Group, a 501(c)3 provides support, advocacy, and resources to new mothers in Montana. PRG will be launching our Network Doula Program, which will send trained postpartum doulas into new mothers homes providing much needed support. Postpartum Resource Group

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: For the Midwife
excerpt:
Dear Midwife,

The words that come to mind after a challenging pregnancy are that you are my;

Friend First. Midwife Second. 


Not something most get the luxury of saying, and likely for good reason. 


> “I don’t become friends with my patients.” 
My midwife tells me years into our relationship
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:
How am I so lucky that I get to call this handsome and kind man my husband?
Part luck maybe, but I'm going to chalk it up to a lot of prayer.

Everytime I put on these brown boots I’m in awe of the love story God designed.

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:
Dear Taylor Swift,


When your first 2020 surprise album dropped, I remember my husband coming into

> “Taylor Swift dropped a new album!”
Being in the midst of an awful Hyperemesis (HG) and Gestational Diabetes

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

The surprise was glorious.

"It's a BOY!" My husband exclaimed. 

Through the post birth fog, I nodded.

"But is baby HEALTHY?"

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

It may sound absurd but I didn't fully understand how sick I felt the last

I'm not sure if it is a coping mechanism but I notice a lot of fellow HG women

It was the easiest of my three deliveries but the life-threatening Postpartum

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:
Dear Armani, 

You taught me that even if I fall off and break my collarbone, I am strong

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:
An ultrasound picture sits in front of his computer, next to a photo taken on

He proudly replaces a picture of us skiing from his phone's home screen with the

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Northern Lights
excerpt:
“This is what you need to do. Send your mom flowers and escape to the woods.” My
grief counselor told me.


It was Mother’s Day circa 2015. It had been a year since losing our baby Selah,
a year filled with continued loss. 

> 
I was broken. 

Mother’s Day
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Grace in Healing
excerpt:
A sunny afternoon, fresh margaritas, an easy conversation on our porch at our
new Montana home. 

A seemingly innocent question,

>  “when are you planning on having children?” 
An inquiry that would send daggers through my heart. 

As a woman facing infertility, pregnancy loss, how exactly do you answer that
question?
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Revelations of Light Fixtures
excerpt:
“You know if you were to update your light fixtures, you could add value?”  A

To me our home is priceless. Set against a quiet hillside, wood-burning
fireplace, a window above our sink, a similar sunken living room to the one
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Learning to Love ME
excerpt:
There are 100 things I’d rather do than hold a baby. Catch a rooster. Ride a
horse. Fly fish. Camp. Have a philosophical conversation. Discover a new Excel
formula. Drink a beer. Dance. Listen to accounting podcasts.  Eat sushi. Hike a
mountain. Reconcile bank accounts. Jump in a lake.
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Prayer Beads of Peace
excerpt:
I’ve got my prayer beads on, a board filled with encouragement from my
community, my Cheers (to NO more nausea) sweatshirt, and we are ready to FINALLY
meet this sweet baby girl. 

I MADE IT 37 WEEKS!
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: THIS is Community
excerpt:
This is LOVING WELL:

 * home-cooked dinners dropped off randomly for nine-months
 * friends bringing groceries
 * homemade ginger-ale prepared with the small hope it might bring some nausea
   relief 
 * prayed over hand knit blankets
 * offers to do our laundry
 * handmade baby girl bows
 * care packages from a far
 * belly casts
 * prayer beads
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Our Pot of Gold
excerpt:
This baby girl is definitely going to be our pot of gold at the end of a

A rainbow brought on after a long LONG storm.

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

We didn't find out what we were having with our boys but with how hard this

Braden has always been anti-gender reveal party, because, you know the ones that
go horribly wrong and almost burn
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

It has been a tough pregnancy so far. At 5 weeks I got hyperemesis gravidarum,

Thankfully I’ve been getting IV fluids and meds every other day which has been a

title: The Blood Clot
excerpt:
Dear Blood Clot,


I began calling you the evil clot that tried to kill me.


The further away I get, I’m beginning to see that maybe it was exactly what I
needed.


I had been in denial for nine months of how sick I was. Forcing myself to camp,
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

I instantly feel bad, “how have I not seen this blue boat that sings silly

How do I not know every single bit of their world.

I am a mom with a career I love.

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt: One day we will all dance in the shadow of the Almighty in heaven but for now we can dance here on earth.

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:
To the woman who raised my husband.

I told you at our wedding that I knew he had become the type of man I was
honored to marry because he was loved by a mom like you.

As a mother now, I understand how true those words really are. If
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Red Spool of Thread
excerpt:
The red spool of thread stares at me.

I am on week 3 of quarantine. With a cough, fever and limited testing, I have

My Grandma wasn’t always the kindest. In fact, her last words to me after
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Tears in the Target Parking Lot
excerpt:
I cried in the Target parking lot.

It wasn’t due to a lack of toilet paper, it wasn’t even in the last 5 years.

I was sitting in my then fiancés Suburau. We had just finished our wedding
registry. New towels, new dishware, new bedding.

And I cried.
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: The Balance Beam
excerpt:
They’re always watching.

I know this but I am still surprised when I see it.

We had 20 youth group kids over to our home last night and cooked them an
Italian feast. Yes 20!

I’ve learned that life is like a balance beam and if you try
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: The Funk
excerpt:
> I was in a funk


I was not going to attend the YoungLife yearly sledding competition, but decided
to pull myself together and make it a Mommy-Son Date with my firstborn.

You know those days where you just feel blah for no reason at all? That was me.

But THIS
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: My Fierce Friend
excerpt:
> “Goodbye my fierce friend.”


We said goodbye to Jocelyn, a fixture of my childhood and my moms fiercely
beautiful friend.

So long Jocelyn, you will forever be an example of showing up and loving
fiercely those who grace your life.
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:
The blue shoes sit quietly on the dining room table. Cheerios are tossed

Both boys are quiet now, tucked into their beds.

You can’t predict the level of noise that will fall upon your home the moment

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

Hyperemesis Gravidarum (HG). Gestational Diabetes (GD). Postpartum Hemorrhage

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:
Her blazer still hangs in my closet. A reminder of a life gone too soon. It

life.

It’s not my style. Sparkly, cream, blazer. During our cross-country move, spring

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

It was a joy to camp, read a book under the stars, and enjoy a beautiful sunrise

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

Difference Makers Live Among Us

I was traveling with my son Gallagher, a busy and loud 14-month-old. I was

After a tiring walk through the airport, baby G strapped to my chest, we sat

author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

excerpt:

Lyrics play in the background.

It’s a song by Jonathan David Helser, an artist that played at the World Race

Feeding my new daughter, she fusses as I whisper, “God never gave
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: An Eternal Grief
excerpt:
We have been visiting this woman since Lachlan was a baby. She always enjoyed

I was humbled by her vulnerability to give me a glimpse into her story.

She has two daughters but always wanted a
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: January Second
excerpt:
January 2nd.

To most that date doesn’t mean anything. To me, it means another year old. A
daughter we never got to hold. A daughter who would be 4-years-old.

Who would you have been Selah?

I see bouncing blond curls. I see big brown eyes. I see her in
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Motherhood Recognized
excerpt:
I can still feel the yearning, the longing to be a mom. I remember vividly the
Mothers Day’s I spent aching for the baby I carried in my body for only so long.

On this day, with my arms full, my heart still aches for those whose laps are
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Heavy Silence
excerpt:
Today I am mourning the loss of Michael.

Lachlan and I had been visiting him for a few weeks. Today when we walked into
his room it was empty. His bed stripped, his personal items gone. Nothing left
but his old dresser and Television.

He was in his early 50’
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Love Boldly
excerpt:
I met a man at an accounting conference, he was the keynote speaker, an author.
I found him intriguing. Taking a chance, I boldly invited him to come stay with
us in Montana. An offer he would surprisingly take me up on.

From outward appearance we are very different--50’s
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Exhausted in the Accomplishment
excerpt:
Camping with littles is hard. Two under two, some would call me crazy but I feel
accomplished and exhausted when we pull back up our driveway.

Hearing their laughs by a campfire, watching them throw rocks into the lake as
the sunsets. Grandma walking them through the wildflowers. Phones and
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Beauty
excerpt:
I was traveling for work.

It's always hard being away from you kids, especially when they're so young.

I'd be remiss to say I don't enjoy some sense of independence but the longing
for my children in my arms always remains.

The sun, the flowers, the babies. My handsome man
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Eternal Words
excerpt:
A year ago a nurse asked if we wanted her to hold our new baby after an
exhausting night.

Gently rocking our boy in the corner, Braden replied,

> “No thanks—there’s nothing better than holding your son on the day he is born.”


There are moments, words, that will
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: The Red Jacket
excerpt:
In a different place, a different time, I imagine this boys dad wearing the same
red jacket.

Sitting at a high chair, waiting for his breakfast. His life set before him.

> Who will he become?
Where will he travel? Who will he marry?


These questions are still left unanswered for
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Camping with Littles
excerpt:
Camping adventure weekend!

I spent two nights in my hammock, Braden and the boys sleeping in our new
adventure mobile.

It was the early morning kayak ride with my two year old son, Lachlan. The glass
mountain lake perfection.

Littles may wake up early, but enjoying the peace of nature
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Traffic
excerpt:
I used to spend two hours a day in that traffic.

I was working downtown Minneapolis and commuting from my hometown of White Bear
Lake. I had just gotten back from a year long backpacking trip doing mission
work around the world.

Sleeping on cement floors, eating simple meals and
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Basking in the Beauty
excerpt:
You now span my entire torso when you fall asleep on my chest but you still have
that new baby smell, that new baby peace.

There’s work to be done, a job that needs my attention, but for this short
moment,

> I will bask in the beauty of you
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Imperfection made Perfect
excerpt:
There are dishes in the sink, laundry to be washed and toys scattered about, but

The perfect sound of Gallaghers laugh, the just right feeling of home, the
flawless smile of my son.

> I realize that it is sitting in
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Celebrating my Motherhood
excerpt:
Celebrating two years as a mom with the woman who I made a mother, Mary Jane

After becoming a mother, you begin to understand the sacrifice that is
motherhood. The diapers changed, tears wiped, tantrums survived, sleepless
nights and the all consuming love.

If we want to celebrate birth
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: The Best is Yet to Come
excerpt:
Five years ago Braden asked me to marry him in the middle of the deserted woods

proposal.

It included a letter in the journal we had passed back and forth while dating, a
playlist of songs to
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: My Hands are Full
excerpt:
The toddler tears are plenty, the whines of wanting to go outside. Off the couch

> For only a season will I put out my hand to have it quickly filled with that of
a smaller one created by me.


Amidst the tantrums of a little
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Due February 2017!
excerpt:
I trudged up the hill behind our house weeping on what would have been the due
date of our first baby when I heard God whisper
"in due time you will give birth to a child." It was a promise God continued to
whisper even as we faced another miscarriage
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Triumph by Testimony
excerpt:
> They TRIUMPHED over him by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their
TESTIMONY; they did not love their lives so much as to shrink from death.
Revelation 12:11


God is good. Jesus is GOOD. People have told me they were amazed that despite my
pain
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: Our Baby Selah
excerpt:
We have decided to name our baby Selah.

The book Heaven describes a little boys experience in Heaven. There is one part
in particular that hits close to home. The little boy tells his mom he met his
other sister in heaven. His mom is astonished, having never told her
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness

title: One in Heaven
excerpt:
“Do you have kids?” A familiar question innocently asked.

Only days after losing our first child, a woman asked us this. Not knowing how
to respond we simply shook our heads no.

Many may not consider us parents, considering the first moment you hold your
child the initiation into parenthood.
author: Margaret LaVigne Rogness
